ticket.description:
Hi Team,

I would like to point out an issue.

Please note that when the label is generated for order 1670, the description section shows the Reach RS4 and survey pole as if they are together as one. This is in correct. Since RS4 contains battery it should also add it to the label.

Could you please fix this?

------------------
Submitted from: https://mcsl-s.pluginhive.io/home/views/orders/summary/26fac6e9-0a4e-4829-a99e-2267e2c2c90e/f5f78e4c-52e6-4c5b-8ff9-e86f856075e2/new

comments.body:
Hi Team,

I would like to point out an issue.

Please note that when the label is generated for order 1670, the description section shows the Reach RS4 and survey pole as if they are together as one. This is in correct. Since RS4 contains battery it should also add it to the label.

Could you please fix this?

------------------

comments.body:
Hi Valeria,

Greetings from PluginHive!

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.

Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.

Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:

1. Click on the Order ID.
2. Go to Packaging Summary.
3. Click Edit.
4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
5. Click Update.
6.

Then generate the label again.



Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance.

Let us know if you need any further assistance.


 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/EEBCDhZa4Uwzi9NZFumerf3dV/?name=Screenshot+2026-03-09+at+4.30.28%E2%80%AFPM.png)
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Hi Jozsef,

Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is externally fulfilled, and hence we are unable to test the label generation for this order.

Could you please share another order number and keep the order in Processing status, so we can try generating the label from our end and check this further?

Looking forward to your response.

Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Hi Valeria,

Greetings from PluginHive!
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

     

Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance.
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance.
 
 

Best regards,

Hi Valeria,

Greetings from PluginHive!
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

     

Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance.
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance.
 
 

Best regards,

Team PluginHive

Please note that when the label is generated for order 1670, the description section shows the Reach RS4 and survey pole as if they are together as one. This is in correct. Since RS4 contains battery it should also add it to the label.

 Hi Valeria, 
 
 
 
Greetings from PluginHive! 
 
  
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility. 
 
  
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status. 
 
  
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below: 
 
 
   
  Click on the Order ID.  
  Go to Packaging Summary.  
  Click Edit.  
  Delete the product you do not want in the package.  
  Click Update.  
   Then generate the label again.      
  Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance. 
 
  
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance. 
 
  
 
  
 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

Hi Team,

Please note that the issue still exists in PH. The label is being generated incorrectly. Since the item we are shipping contains a battery, this should be reflected in the description section as **PI 967 SECTION**. I have downloaded the label and attached it for your reference.

Could you please advise what might be causing the system to generate the label incorrectly?

This is the correct format it should look like:

 ![](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/qY7qSNmafENEPIKaG0umM3bav/?name=Screenshot+2026-03-16+at+13.15.12.png)

Thank you for looking into this.

 --

 Jozsef Lakatos

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

Greetings from PluginHive! 
 
  
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility. 
 
  
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status. 
 
  
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below: 
 
 
   
  Click on the Order ID.  
  Go to Packaging Summary.  
  Click Edit.  
  Delete the product you do not want in the package.  
  Click Update.  
   Then generate the label again.      
  Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance. 
 
  
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance.

    Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)     Mar 9, 2026, 16:31 GMT+5:30   
  
 Hi Valeria, 
 
 
 
Greetings from PluginHive! 
 
  
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility. 
 
  
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status. 
 
  
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below: 
 
 
   
  Click on the Order ID.  
  Go to Packaging Summary.  
  Click Edit.  
  Delete the product you do not want in the package.  
  Click Update.  
   Then generate the label again.      
  Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance. 
 
  
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance. 
 
  
 
  
 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

comments.body:
Call summary :
The customer would like the custom description to be printed on the shipping label, as it contains important battery instructions.
However, the customer reported that when they generate the label, the custom description is not being printed, whereas when their colleague generates the label, it appears correctly.
The issue has been observed for the following orders:

- Order 1694
- Order 1630
This needs to be further checked with the team, Keeping it open

  Go to Packaging Summary.

  Click Update.

I discussed this with team:

The customer would like the custom description to be printed on the shipping label, as it contains important battery instructions.
However, the customer reported that for some orders/ products it's printed and for some its not printed.

For order 1630, the Custom Description : PI 967 Section II compliant is printed
In the request, it is being passed as well.

For order 1694, the Custom Description: PI 967 Section II compliant is not printed on the label and on the request it's not being passed as well.

Need to check with customer if we can go ahead and generate a label for an order containing "Reach RS3" product

Keeping on hold

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

Greetings from PluginHive! 
 
  
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility. 
 
  
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status. 
 
  
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below: 
 
 
   
  Click on the Order ID.  
  Go to Packaging Summary.  
  Click Edit.  
  Delete the product you do not want in the package.  
  Click Update.  
   Then generate the label again.      
  Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance. 
 
  
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance. 
 
  
 
  
 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

Greetings from PluginHive! 
 
  
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility. 
 
  
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status. 
 
  
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below: 
 
 
   
  Click on the Order ID.  
  Go to Packaging Summary.  
  Click Edit.  
  Delete the product you do not want in the package.  
  Click Update.  
   Then generate the label again.      
  Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance. 
 
  
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance. 
 
  
 
  
 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka

Hi Jozsef,

I wanted to inform you that the Shopify account is associated with b2b@emlid.com, and therefore, we can only send the Collaborator Access request to that email. Please accept the request from that account to proceed.

Additionally, could you please unfulfill Order 1694 in Shopify so we can continue with the necessary checks and actions from our end?

Looking forward to your confirmation.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

Let us know if you need any further assistance.

Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility.
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status.
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below:
  1. Click on the Order ID.
  2. Go to Packaging Summary.
  3. Click Edit.
  4. Delete the product you do not want in the package.
  5. Click Update.

  6. Then generate the label again.

     

Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance.
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance.
 
 

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

 Hi Valeria, 
 
 
 
Greetings from PluginHive! 
 
  
 
Could you please let us know how you would like the description to appear on the label? If possible, please share an example so we can better understand your requirement and check the feasibility. 
 
  
 
Upon checking, I can see that order 1670 is currently in Processing status. 
 
  
 
Additionally, if you would like only one product to appear on the label, you can remove the other product from the package details and then generate the label again by following the steps below: 
 
 
   
  Click on the Order ID.  
  Go to Packaging Summary.  
  Click Edit.  
  Delete the product you do not want in the package.  
  Click Update.  
   Then generate the label again.      
  Please refer to the attached screenshot for guidance. 
 
  
 
Let us know if you need any further assistance. 
 
  
 
  
 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
The customer would like the custom description to be printed on the shipping label, as it contains important battery instructions.
However, the customer reported that for some orders/ products it's printed and for some its not printed.

For order #1717, there are two products.
Reach RS3 and Survey Pole with a smartphone mount however the custom description is being printed only for 1 product.  PI 967 Section II compliant ( Reach RS3 ) is not printed on the label and on the request it's being passed.

Need to check with team

However, the customer reported that for some orders/ products it's printed and for some its not printed.

For order #1717, there are two products.

comments.body:
UPS allows only 50 characters in Description field, the first 50 characters of the description will be passed in the request. If merchant wants to display all product description, it is advised to add smaller descriptions.
[Veena]

comments.body:
Hi Jozsef,

Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.

We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.

This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.

To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.

Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Hi Team,

I would like to point out that this information appears to be incorrect, as it works perfectly fine for my colleague. We have double-checked, and when I download the label, the description is added incorrectly, whereas when she downloads the same label, the description appears correctly.

Previously, I shared a couple of examples showing how the description should appear on the label. Since the item we are shipping contains a battery, **this should be reflected in the description section as PI 967 Section.**

I also shared another label where I attempted to ship 8× Reach RX2 units (which contain batteries). If you open that label and check the description section, you will see that PI 967 Section is not included.

The label below corresponds to our test order 1717, which I have already shared with you. As you can see, the description is incorrect.

 ![](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/E5bI55IIMvjZ0CtlhD4FOgNkM/?name=Screenshot+2026-03-25+at+10.54.52.png)

 ![](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/7UfK0uPZPZLLJX3dQW7wzIC62/?name=Screenshot+2026-03-25+at+10.57.24.png)

comments.html_body:
Hi Team,


I would like to point out that this information appears to be incorrect, as it works perfectly fine for my colleague. We have double-checked, and when I download the label, the description is added incorrectly, whereas when she downloads the same label, the description appears correctly.


Previously, I shared a couple of examples showing how the description should appear on the label. Since the item we are shipping contains a battery, this should be reflected in the description section as PI 967 Section.


I also shared another label where I attempted to ship 8× Reach RX2 units (which contain batteries). If you open that label and check the description section, you will see that PI 967 Section is not included.


The label below corresponds to our test order 1717, which I have already shared with you. As you can see, the description is incorrect.




To give an example, this would be a correct description:




--
Jozsef Lakatos
Sales Support Specialist
Emlid



On Tue, Mar 24, 2026 at 5:33 PM, Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive) <support@pluginhive.com> wrote:

You are registered as a CC on this request (377795). Reply to this email to add a comment to the request.

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Hi Team,  
  
  
  I would like to point out that this information appears to be incorrect, as it works perfectly fine for my colleague. We have double-checked, and when I download the label, the description is added incorrectly, whereas when she downloads the same label, the description appears correctly.  
  
  
  Previously, I shared a couple of examples showing how the description should appear on the label. Since the item we are shipping contains a battery, this should be reflected in the description section as PI 967 Section.  
  
  
  I also shared another label where I attempted to ship 8× Reach RX2 units (which contain batteries). If you open that label and check the description section, you will see that PI 967 Section is not included.  
  
  
  The label below corresponds to our test order 1717, which I have already shared with you. As you can see, the description is incorrect.

    Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)     Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30   
  
 Hi Jozsef, 
 
  
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team. 
 
  
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label. 
 
  
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order 
 #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products. 
 
  
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit. 
 
  
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

Additionally, we would like to review this together with you on a quick call to better understand the behavior and assist you accordingly. Please feel free to book a convenient time using the link below. 
 
[ 
 Book a call] 
 
  
 
If you have any other details to share in the meantime, please let us know and we’ll be happy to assist. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Jozsef,

Thank you for sharing the screenshots and examples.
I would like to inform you that from the details you shared, Order 1717 contains two products:

- Survey Pole with a smartphone mount – custom description: _"Survey Pole with a smartphone mount"_
- Reach RS3 – custom description: _"PI 967 Section II compliant"_
As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.

Regarding the second screenshot you shared, it corresponds to Order 1630, which contains only one product; hence, the description is fully printed.

We would be happy to explain this further over a call and guide you through it. Please book a convenient time using the link below:
[Book a call]

Looking forward to assisting you.

Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

comments.html_body:
Hi Team,


I have reviewed previous orders from my colleague, specifically order 1687, which included the following items:

  • 4× HND101-P

  • 6× SPL102

  • 4× RCH401 (battery devices)

If you open the labels, they clearly show the correct descriptions. I would like to have the same format for our labels so we can ship orders without any issues. As you can see, the labels only provide the description for the battery unit and do not mention the accessory units.



Can we fix this issue?


--
Jozsef Lakatos
Sales Support Specialist
Emlid

Your request (377795) has been updated. To add additional comments, reply to this email.

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 25, 2026, 17:43 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for sharing the screenshots and examples.
I would like to inform you that from the details you shared, Order 1717 contains two products:
  • Survey Pole with a smartphone mount – custom description: "Survey Pole with a smartphone mount"
  • Reach RS3 – custom description: "PI 967 Section II compliant"
As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
Regarding the second screenshot you shared, it corresponds to Order 1630, which contains only one product; hence, the description is fully printed.
 
We would be happy to explain this further over a call and guide you through it. Please book a convenient time using the link below:
[ Book a call]
 
Looking forward to assisting you.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit. 
 
  
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

> > > >
> > > > We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
> > > >
> > > > This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
> > > >
> > > > To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
> > > >
> > > > Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.

Hi Team,


I have reviewed previous orders from my colleague, specifically order 1687, which included the following items:

  • 4× HND101-P

  • 6× SPL102

  • 4× RCH401 (battery devices)

If you open the labels, they clearly show the correct descriptions. I would like to have the same format for our labels so we can ship orders without any issues. As you can see, the labels only provide the description for the battery unit and do not mention the accessory units.



Can we fix this issue?

Mar 25, 2026, 17:43 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for sharing the screenshots and examples.
I would like to inform you that from the details you shared, Order 1717 contains two products:
  • Survey Pole with a smartphone mount – custom description: "Survey Pole with a smartphone mount"
  • Reach RS3 – custom description: "PI 967 Section II compliant"
As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
Regarding the second screenshot you shared, it corresponds to Order 1630, which contains only one product; hence, the description is fully printed.
 
We would be happy to explain this further over a call and guide you through it. Please book a convenient time using the link below:
[ Book a call]
 
Looking forward to assisting you.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

Hi Team,


I would like to point out that this information appears to be incorrect, as it works perfectly fine for my colleague. We have double-checked, and when I download the label, the description is added incorrectly, whereas when she downloads the same label, the description appears correctly.


Previously, I shared a couple of examples showing how the description should appear on the label. Since the item we are shipping contains a battery, this should be reflected in the description section as PI 967 Section.


I also shared another label where I attempted to ship 8× Reach RX2 units (which contain batteries). If you open that label and check the description section, you will see that PI 967 Section is not included.


The label below corresponds to our test order 1717, which I have already shared with you. As you can see, the description is incorrect.

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

 Hi Jozsef, 
 
  
 
Thank you for sharing the screenshots and examples. 
 
I would like to inform you that from the details you shared, Order 1717 contains two products: 
 
 
   
  Survey Pole with a smartphone mount – custom description: "Survey Pole with a smartphone mount"  
  Reach RS3 – custom description: "PI 967 Section II compliant"  
  As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label. 
 
  
 
Regarding the second screenshot you shared, it corresponds to Order 1630, which contains only one product; hence, the description is fully printed. 
 
  
 
We would be happy to explain this further over a call and guide you through it. Please book a convenient time using the link below:

    Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)     Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30   
  
 Hi Jozsef, 
 
  
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team. 
 
  
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label. 
 
  
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order 
 #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products. 
 
  
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit. 
 
  
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

> > > > > Hi Jozsef,
> > > > >
> > > > > Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
> > > > >
> > > > > We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
> > > > >
> > > > > This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
> > > > >
> > > > > To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
> > > > >
> > > > > Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
> > > > >
> > > > > Best regards,
> > > > > Priyanka
> > > > > Team PluginHive

  • Reach RS3 – custom description: "PI 967 Section II compliant"
As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
Regarding the second screenshot you shared, it corresponds to Order 1630, which contains only one product; hence, the description is fully printed.

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

We would be happy to explain this further over a call and guide you through it. Please book a convenient time using the link below: 
 
[ 
 Book a call] 
 
  
 
Looking forward to assisting you. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

    Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)     Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30   
  
 Hi Jozsef, 
 
  
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team. 
 
  
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label. 
 
  
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order 
 #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products. 
 
  
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit. 
 
  
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive

> > > > > > Hi Jozsef,

> > > > > > Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
> > > > > >
> > > > > > We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
> > > > > >
> > > > > > This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
> > > > > >
> > > > > > To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
> > > > > >
> > > > > > Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
> > > > > >
> > > > > > Best regards,

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Query Details:
The customer requires the custom description to be printed on the shipping label, as it includes important battery-related instructions.

However, they have observed that:

- When there is a single product, the custom description is printed correctly.
-

When there are multiple products, the custom description is not printed on the label.



An L3 was raised earlier:
https://pluginhive.slack.com/archives/C02E15X8X0C/p1774349347982819
As per L3, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field, and only the first 50 characters are passed in the request. It was suggested to keep descriptions shorter if needed.
However, the issue persists even when the total description is within 50 characters, and it is still not being printed on the label for multiple-product orders. Hence, we are reaching out to UPS for further clarification.

The customer has shared their UPS contact:
lino.towikromo@europe.ups.com

They have also provided the following order IDs for testing:

- Order #1740
- Order #1739

We are currently awaiting update from usps team on https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/agent/tickets/381716
Keeping it open

  • Survey Pole with a smartphone mount – custom description: "Survey Pole with a smartphone mount"
  • Reach RS3 – custom description: "PI 967 Section II compliant"
As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
Regarding the second screenshot you shared, it corresponds to Order 1630, which contains only one product; hence, the description is fully printed.

Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)

Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30

Hi Jozsef,
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team.
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products.
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit.
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions.
 
Best regards,
Priyanka
Team PluginHive

  Survey Pole with a smartphone mount – custom description: "Survey Pole with a smartphone mount"  
  Reach RS3 – custom description: "PI 967 Section II compliant"  
  As mentioned earlier, UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label.

    Priyanka Mukhia (PluginHive)     Mar 24, 2026, 22:03 GMT+5:30   
  
 Hi Jozsef, 
 
  
 
Thank you for your patience while we checked this with our team. 
 
  
 
We would like to inform you that UPS allows only up to 50 characters in the Description field on the shipping label. Due to this limitation, only the first 50 characters of the product description are passed in the request and printed on the label. 
 
  
 
This is the reason why, in cases like Order 
 #1717, the full custom description is not appearing for all products. 
 
  
 
To ensure that the required information (such as battery instructions) is displayed consistently, we recommend using a shortened description within the 50-character limit. 
 
  
 
Please feel free to reach out if you need any assistance in optimizing the description or have any further questions. 
 
  
 
Best regards, 
 
Priyanka 
 
Team PluginHive
